body_txt: A character gets a "cigar", puts it into his mouth, lights it, and... KA-BOOM! Another victim of the Explosive Cigar ! This sort of thing commonly occurs in cartoons. In a comedic work, after the smoke clears, the poor sap will have a smoke-blackened face , their eyes will be bugging out in alarm and the cigar will now be a stub with a star-shape of peeled-back cigar paper at the end. In more serious works, the results will typically end up being fatal . A common variation of this joke is similar to the Poisoned Chalice Switcheroo : the victim suspects the cigar is "loaded" and insists on swapping cigars with the instigator; unfortunately, the instigator is too clever and had the "loaded" one in the first place, meaning the victim still ends up falling for it. A form of Non-Fatal Explosions . Also see Ash Face . Compare Eat the Bomb . Contrast Cigar-Fuse Lighting . See also Tainted Tobacco for a more subtle way to kill with a cigar.
